title: Bug: MCP update_task treats empty-string optional params as provided
description:
Investigate MCP compatibility issue where update_task appears to treat empty-string optional params (e.g. old_description/new_description/parentId) as provided instead of omitted, causing mutual-exclusion or reparent validation errors.

Observed symptom:
- Attempting to call update_task with a new description failed with errors like:
  - "cannot use description with old_description/new_description"
  - "Cannot reparent under : not your task or descendant"
- This suggests the MCP layer or request parser serializes empty strings and backend validation treats them as set.

Need to determine whether the bug is in:
1. MCP client/tool serialization
2. server-side schema parsing/defaulting
3. update_task operation wrapper

Why:
This is exactly the kind of MCP compatibility rough edge that can break agent orchestration and makes tool behavior differ from expectations.
